message:
My neighbor Karen has a laminated escape plan taped to every bedroom door. I used to think it was a *tiny* overkill. Then our kitchen got a little too friendly with the stove one Tuesday night, and I realized I didn't actually know the fastest way out of my own house without thinking about it.

Turns out Karen was onto something. Not the laminating (okay, maybe a little), but the *actually knowing*. We walked through it together—front door, back door, where to meet outside, making sure the kids could find their way even in the dark or smoke.

Now I'm not saying I'm going full Karen with the label maker, but I'm definitely grateful she normalized the slightly nerdy stuff that turns out to matter.

Have you and your family ever actually walked through an escape plan, or is it more of a "we'd probably figure it out" situation? 🏠
